steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a transitions question. Sentence 1 establishes that natural selection was an “obvious next step” given ideas already circulating in the 1800s. Sentence 2 notes that Darwin and Wallace may not have independently discovered it by coincidence. The blank links these two sentences: Sentence 2 is a logical consequence of Sentence 1 — if the idea was obvious, independent discovery follows naturally.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘however,’: “However” signals contrast. Sentence 2 does not contradict Sentence 1; it reinforces it. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘then,’: “Then” here means “in that case” or “it follows that,” expressing a logical inference from Sentence 1. If natural selection was the obvious next step, then of course two scientists would arrive at it simultaneously. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘moreover,’: “Moreover” adds an additional, separate point. Sentence 2 is not a separate addition; it is the expected consequence of Sentence 1’s claim. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘for example,’: “For example” requires Sentence 2 to be a specific instance that illustrates Sentence 1. Sentence 2 is a consequence, not an illustration. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
